text: After the right atriotomy, the first step of the operation is to place the ring stitches.
This allows a perfect exposure and assessment of the tricuspid valve.
Then we place stitches to deploy the anterior leaflet and to perform a safe section of the leaflet itself that should be done not close to the free margin and should go from commissure to commissure.
Four stitches are subsequently placed at the cardinal points of the opening and sizing of the patch is done.
The patch should be loose enough and large enough to account for the tissue loss during suturing.
It is important to remember that while tying, the assistant should hold the two extremities of the suturing line in order to avoid any pursing effect that may shrink the patch itself.
A so-called dirty water test is done for a preliminary assessment of the result.
Therefore, the sizing of the ring is done
In general we use a 28 ring.
